preview_text:
The model forecasts France as a heavy favourite, assigning a 80% win probability. Sweden's chance of victory stands at just 2%, positioning the visitors as substantial underdogs in this neutral-venue fixture.

The win-draw-loss distribution reflects a wide gap between the teams. France's dominance leaves limited room for competitive outcomes; draws carry an 18% probability, while Sweden's path to victory appears narrow. The match is not expected to be closely matched—France enters as a commanding favourite.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge on the underdog. The model credits Sweden with only a 2% win probability, whereas the market consensus assigns 10%, a gap of approximately 8 percentage points. Polymarket prices sit between the two, at 8%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is correct; the divergence may reflect differing weights applied to squad depth, variance, or other unmeasured factors. On France's win probability, all three sources cluster tightly around 75–80%.
